item.title: Hasbro CEO Chris Cocks Discusses AI, KPop Demon Hunters, and the Future of Harry Potter
item.description: Hasbro CEO Chris Cocks discusses the company's use of AI in toy design, the shift towards adult-oriented products, and its partnership with the Harry Potter franchise.

item.title: Vlad Tenev's Take on Prediction Markets
item.description: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev discusses prediction markets, the Iran conflict, and its potential impact on global markets in a recent podcast interview.

item.title: US Bombers Arrive at RAF Fairford Amid Conflict with Iran
item.description: US bombers have landed at RAF Fairford in Gloucestershire as the UK approved 'defensive' US action against Iranian missile sites from UK bases.

item.title: Abu Dhabi's Judan Acquires Majority Stake in Alpha Wave Global
item.description: Abu Dhabi's Judan acquires a 50.1% stake in Alpha Wave Global, an alternative asset manager with investments in SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI.

item.title: G7 Considers Tapping Emergency Oil Reserves Amid Global Price Surge
item.description: G7 finance ministers consider releasing 300 million barrels of emergency oil reserves to temper the global price surge, but concerns surrounding naval escorts and insurance remain.

item.title: iPhone 17E: Is the Extra Cost Worth It?
item.description: The iPhone 17E offers several upgrades over its predecessor, but the question remains whether the additional cost is justified. If you have the means, the iPhone 17 may be a better option.

item.title: 2027 Chevy Bolt Returns With a Twist, Leaving Fans Both Excited and Disappointed
item.description: The 2027 Chevrolet Bolt has made a comeback after an 18-month production gap, but it's not the same as before. The new model has undergone significant changes, sacrificing some features, including CarPlay and Android Auto.

item.title: Panic's Gaming Ambitions Hinge on Unconventional Projects
item.description: Panic, the publisher behind Untitled Goose Game, is set to release Big Walk, a co-op multiplayer game that follows the success of its offbeat titles. The company's gaming business is now bigger than its Mac software business.

item.title: Apple iPad Air M4 Review: Faster, But Worth the Upgrade?
item.description: The Apple iPad Air M4 review reveals a device with upgraded chips and a competitive price, but with some compromises.

item.title: How the Iran War Could Affect Your Daily Life
item.description: The Iran war could lead to higher petrol and diesel prices, increased shipping costs, and food shortages, impacting household budgets and the overall economy.

item.title: Flipkart Moves Headquarters Back to India Ahead of IPO
item.description: Flipkart has relocated its headquarters back to India from Singapore as it prepares for a potential IPO in the coming months, aiming for a stock market debut in India in the financial year ending March 2027.

item.title: Keir Starmer Warns of Economic Impact of Iran Conflict on UK
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer warns of the economic impact of the Iran conflict on the UK, saying the longer it lasts, the more likely it is to affect the economy. The prime minister understands people's anxiety and is working with international partners to reduce the impact.

item.title: Live Nation Accused of Concert Industry Terror
item.description: A core theme in the antitrust trial against Live Nation-Ticketmaster is retaliation, with witnesses testifying that the company withheld concerts from venues that didn't use Ticketmaster.

item.title: US-Israeli Conflict in Iran: Impact on Oil Prices
item.description: The conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran has pushed up oil prices, with the price of crude oil rising to over $100 a barrel. The US military has sunk an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean, and the conflict has escalated with strikes being traded between opposing sides.

item.title: Tributes Paid to Grime Pioneer Dot Rotten After Death at 37
item.description: Grime pioneer Dot Rotten has died at the age of 37, with tributes pouring in from the music industry.

item.title: Eyewitness Recounts Vape Shop Fire That Led to Inferno
item.description: An eyewitness recounted his attempt to tackle a fire at a vape shop near Glasgow Central station, which led to an inferno that engulfed the building, causing partial collapse and widespread destruction.

item.title: New Zealand Charity Shop Receives Accidental Donation of Cannabis and Cash
item.description: Two 16-year-old teenagers were arrested in New Zealand after accidentally leaving a backpack containing cannabis and thousands of dollars in cash outside a charity shop.

item.title: UK Petrol and Diesel Prices Could Surge Amid US-Israel War with Iran
item.description: UK motorists face higher fuel costs as the US-Israel war with Iran disrupts global energy markets, leading to a surge in wholesale oil and gas prices.

item.title: Princess Eugenie Departs From Anti-Slavery Charity Amid Epstein Scandal
item.description: Princess Eugenie has stepped down as patron of Anti-Slavery International, a charity she has supported for seven years. The move comes amid renewed scrutiny of her father, Prince Andrew, over his links to Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Lib Dems Urge PM to Call Off King's US State Visit Over Iran War
item.description: The Liberal Democrats are calling on Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to cancel the King's state visit to the US over concerns about President Trump's criticism of the British response to the Iran conflict.

item.title: Middle East Turns to Ukraine for Drone Interceptor Expertise
item.description: Ukraine sends drone experts to Middle East to help Gulf states under attack from Iran, as the region grapples with the threat of Iranian drones.

item.title: Alleged Bondi Gunman Seeks Court Order to Suppress Family's Identity
item.description: Alleged Bondi gunman Naveed Akram seeks court order to suppress family's identity, citing concerns for their safety.

item.title: G7 Nations to Hold Emergency Meeting on Oil Amid Stock Market Slump
item.description: G7 nations will hold an emergency meeting to discuss surging oil prices and the economic impact of the escalating US-Israeli war with Iran, as global oil prices reached nearly $120 and stock markets slumped.

item.title: India's T20 World Cup Hero: The Rise of Sanju Samson
item.description: Sanju Samson scripted the most important chapter of his career when India won the T20 World Cup, with Samson being named the most valuable player of the tournament.

item.title: Australia Urged to Protect Iranian Women's Football Team After Asian Cup Elimination
item.description: Concerns for the safety of Iran's women's football team have grown after they declined to sing the national anthem ahead of their first match against South Korea. Australian authorities are being urged to protect the team as they prepare to fly home from the Asian Cup.

item.title: New Faces Revealed in Bid to Solve Reservoir Mystery
item.description: Police release new images of a man's face in a bid to solve the mystery of his death in a remote Welsh reservoir, 18 months after his body was found in a £200 XXL wetsuit.

item.title: Gerry Adams Faces Civil Court Case Over IRA Bombings
item.description: Gerry Adams faces a civil court case in London over his alleged liability for IRA bombings in 1973 and 1996. The three victims are seeking a ruling that Adams is personally responsible for the attacks.

item.title: Cumbria Firm Develops Predator-Spotting Tech for Women's Safety
item.description: Createc in Cumbria is developing a predator-spotting laser technology to quickly identify and respond to potential threats against women in public spaces. The tool uses laser beams and advanced mathematics to track people and detect unusual patterns of behavior.

item.title: Iranians Describe Ongoing Strikes on Oil Depots Amid US-Israeli Attack
item.description: Iranians describe the strikes on oil depots as 'night turned into day' as the US-Israeli attack on the country continues. The strikes have killed at least 1,332 Iranian civilians and wounded thousands.

item.title: Ring CEO Tries to Alleviate Privacy Fears, but Answers Raise More Questions
item.description: Ring CEO Jamie Siminoff attempts to calm concerns about privacy, but his explanations raise more questions about the company's intentions.
